content.title: Curbs & Gutters
content.tagline: Protecting Your Property, Enhancing Your Street
content.subtitle: Durable, code-compliant curbs and gutters that manage stormwater, improve safety, and boost curb appeal. Installed by Maxima Concrete.

content.title: Why are Curbs and Gutters important?
content.subtitle: Curbs and gutters may not be the first thing you notice, but they’re essential for maintaining the safety, durability, and beauty of your property and neighborhood. Positioned along the edge of the roadway or driveway, these structures are critical for drainage control, pedestrian safety, and defini
content.paragraph1: At Maxima Concrete, we design and install code-compliant curbs and gutters that are both functional and visually refined — ensuring your property looks great while meeting all local regulations.

content.title: The role of Curbs and Gutters

items.text: Curbs and gutters channel rainwater runoff from roofs, driveways, and yards — directing it toward storm drains instead of letting it pool or erode your landscape. This prevents flooding, soil erosion, and road damage, while helping protect nearby sidewalks and driveways.

Curbs act as physical barriers, separating traffic lanes from sidewalks and residential driveways. This creates safe zones for pedestrians, prevents cars from veering off-road, and ensures smoother vehicle flow in residential and commercial areas.

Beyond functionality, curbs help define the edges of driveways and walkways, creating a cleaner visual boundary. They also make parking and maneuvering easier, guiding vehicles while protecting lawns and landscaping.

Well-maintained curbs and gutters enhance curb appeal and signal pride of ownership. Cracked or neglected curbs can create a sense of disrepair — while clean, uniform ones increase property value and contribute to the neighborhood's visual harmony.

content.title: Built to Code, Built to Last

styles.title: Permit management:
styles.description: We pull and pay for local Right-of-Way permits.

styles.description: Expert crews form and pour curbs and gutters to precise grades and dimensions.

styles.title: Quality materials:
styles.description: We use 6.5-bag high-strength concrete, fiber reinforcement, and 10-gauge wire mesh (as needed) for structural integrity.

styles.title: Final inspection:
styles.description: All installations are inspected for code compliance and durability.

content.description: Installing curbs and gutters in Ohio often requires compliance with local municipal codes and proper Right-of-Way permits.

items.question: Do I need a permit to install a curb or gutter?
items.answer: Often yes, especially for work in the public right-of-way. Maxima handles all required permits and ensures the work meets local codes.

items.question: How long do curbs and gutters last?
items.answer: When installed correctly, they can last 30+ years with minimal maintenance.

items.question: Can damaged curbs be repaired or must they be replaced?
items.answer: It depends on the extent of the damage. Minor sections can often be repaired, while severely cracked or heaved curbs are best replaced for lasting results.

items.question: Do curbs really affect property value?
items.answer: Yes. Clean, well-defined curbs and gutters improve drainage, protect your pavement, and boost curb appeal — all of which support property value.
